title: Subgroup/disparity evaluation across aggregate strata (no individual data)
context: Oncology ML benchmarks evaluated only on aggregate metrics can mask systematic performance disparities across demographic or clinical subgroups. Subgroup analysis at the aggregate stratum level (no re-identification, no individual-level data) can surface whether a baseline model performs systematically worse for a subgroup defined by an aggregate covariate (e.g. age band, sex, cancer stage as available in the open/de-identified data). This evaluation uses only aggregate strata present in the already-gated, de-identified benchmark data — no additional individual-level data is collected or linked.
objective: Implement subgroup/disparity evaluation across aggregate strata available in the gated benchmark data: for each baseline model, report metric disaggregation by available aggregate strata (e.g. age band, sex, cancer stage if present in the open/de-identified dataset); flag strata with substantially lower performance; ensure the evaluation uses only aggregate strata and never re-identifies or links to individuals; add disparity reporting to the model-card format.
acceptanceCriteria: Subgroup evaluation uses only aggregate strata present in the already-gated, publisher-de-identified benchmark data; no additional individual-level data is collected, linked, or inferred; any stratum with a cell size below a minimum threshold (to prevent re-identification risk) is suppressed and that suppression is documented

For each baseline model, metrics are disaggregated by available aggregate strata (e.g. age band, sex, cancer stage); strata with substantially lower performance (e.g. more than 10 percentage points below the aggregate metric) are flagged
Disparity reporting is added to the model-card format: each card includes the aggregate strata evaluated, the per-stratum metrics, and any flagged disparities
The evaluation code is added to the harness and runs via the shared audit/metric/provenance code paths (no harness forking)

The evaluation is documented with its limitation: it can surface aggregate disparities but cannot determine their causes without additional domain analysis, and is not a re-identification or individual-level analysis
Domain reviewer and technical reviewer sign-offs on the subgroup evaluation design and suppression threshold are recorded
output: A merged PR adding aggregate-stratum subgroup/disparity evaluation to the harness and model-card format, with suppression of small strata and disparity flagging — extending benchmark honesty to subgroup-level reporting.
